content.problemStatement:
Sonya is a very smart girl, so Danya wants to give her something interesting. He thought about it for a long time and decided that the best gift would be a tree. This tree has NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN); of course, it is a connected graph with N−1N-1N−1 edges connecting those vertices.

Danya realised that an ordinary tree probably would not surprise Sonya. Therefore, he decided to place integers 000 through N−1N-1N−1 into the tree in such a way that each vertex contains exactly one integer and each of these integers appears in exactly one vertex. For each valid iii, let's denote the integer placed in vertex iii by pip_ipi​.

The beauty of a tree is defined as the number of beautiful simple undirected paths in it. A path with length LLL (containing LLL vertices; possibly L=1L=1L=1) is beautiful if for each iii (0≤i≤L−10 \le i \le L-10≤i≤L−1), there is a vertex vvv on this path such that pv=ip_v = ipv​=i.

Danya doubts that this is the most beautiful tree, so he wants to perform MMM operations. In each operation, he selects two (not necessarily distinct) vertices uuu and vvv and swaps pup_upu​ with pvp_vpv​. After each operation, he would like to know the current beauty of the tree. Please help him compute it so that he can decide when the tree will be the best gift for Sonya.
constraints: 0≤pi≤N−10 \le p_i \le N - 10≤pi​≤N−1 for each valid iii
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (33 points):** Degree of each vertex is at most 2
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers p1,p2,…,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1​,p2​,…,pN​ denoting the integers initially placed in the tree.
Each of the next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv.
The next line contains a single integer MMM.
Each of the next MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv describing one operation.
content.outputFormat: For each operation, print a single line containing one integer -  the beauty of the tree after this operation.
